story_title: In Which We Are Introduced to Winnie-the-Pooh and Some Bees, and the Stories Begin
story_summary: Winnie-the-Pooh, a bear who lives in the forest under the name of Sanders, tries to reach the honey at the top of a great oak-tree by floating up on Christopher Robin's blue balloon disguised as a small black cloud — but the bees grow suspicious and turn out to be the wrong sort.

entities.summary: A bear of very little brain who loves honey above all things; he lives in the forest under the name of Sanders.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Here is Edward Bear ... ready to be introduced to you. Winnie-the-Pooh.

entities.summary: Winnie-the-Pooh's dearest friend, a boy who lives in the forest behind a green door and helps Pooh with his honey schemes.
entities.evidence_excerpt: his friend Christopher Robin, who lived behind a green door in another part of the forest

entities.summary: The forest where Winnie-the-Pooh and Christopher Robin live.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Winnie-the-Pooh lived in a forest all by himself under the name of Sanders.

entities.summary: A large oak in an open place in the middle of the forest, with a bees' nest and honey at its top.
entities.evidence_excerpt: in the middle of this place was a large oak-tree, and, from the top of the tree, there came a loud buzzing-noise

entities.summary: The bees nesting in the great oak-tree — as Pooh finally decided, 'the wrong sort of bees.'
entities.evidence_excerpt: These are the wrong sort of bees.

entities.summary: The important bee Pooh most wished to deceive.
entities.evidence_excerpt: The important bee to deceive is the Queen Bee.

entities.summary: The honey at the top of the oak-tree that Pooh schemes to eat.
entities.evidence_excerpt: the only reason for making honey is so as I can eat it

entities.summary: Christopher Robin's big blue balloon, which Pooh used to float up to the bees disguised as a small black cloud.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Then you had better have the blue balloon

entities.summary: Christopher Robin's big green balloon, brought home from Piglet's party.
entities.evidence_excerpt: You had had a big green balloon

entities.summary: Christopher Robin's friend, at whose party the balloons were had. (Mentioned.)
entities.evidence_excerpt: you had been to a party the day before at the house of your friend Piglet

entities.summary: Owner of the relation who left the blue balloon behind. (Mentioned.)
entities.evidence_excerpt: one of Rabbit's relations had had a big blue one

chunks.title: Winnie-the-Pooh
chunks.body: Winnie-the-Pooh — also Edward Bear, or just Pooh — is a bear of very little brain who loves honey above all else. He lives in the forest under the name of Sanders, and his dearest friend is Christopher Robin.

chunks.title: The forest and the great oak
chunks.body: Pooh and Christopher Robin live in the forest. In an open place at its middle stands a great oak-tree, and at the very top of the oak is a bees' nest full of honey.

chunks.title: Pooh and Christopher Robin
chunks.body: Christopher Robin is Winnie-the-Pooh's best friend; he lives behind a green door and gamely helps Pooh with his honey schemes — lending balloons, carrying umbrellas, and shooting the occasional balloon when needed.

chunks.title: The honey is still up the oak
chunks.body: Pooh still means to get the honey at the top of the great oak. Last time the bees turned out to be the wrong sort; he may yet think of a cleverer trick than floating up as a small black cloud.

chunks.title: Continuity: Pooh and Some Bees
chunks.body: Previous story summary: Winnie-the-Pooh tried to reach the honey at the top of the great oak by floating up on Christopher Robin's blue balloon, disguised as a small black cloud. The bees grew suspicious, Pooh decided they were the wrong sort of bees, and Christopher Robin shot the balloon to bring him gently down.

open_questions: What is the right sort of bees, and where would Pooh find the right sort of honey?
